markdown:
#  Do I need to create a dedicated user account in Sage 300 CRE for the hh2 synchronization client? 
##  Answer 
Yes, it is a recommended best practice to always create a dedicated user account when a system service requires login credentials, such as the hh2 Synchronization Client. For the Procore + Sage 300 CRE®, this ensures that the client has authorized login credentials for synchronizing data between hh2 and your Sage 300 CRE® server. Although you could technically use a person's user account, this can lead to future administration problems if something changes with the user's account. For example, if the person were to change their password, the synchronization process would be instantly interrupted.
## See Also
  * [Procore Pay and ERP: Detailed Data Mapping](https://v2.support.procore.com/reference-procore-pay-and-erp-detailed-data-mapping)
  * [How does hh2 connect to the Sage 300 CRE server?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-does-hh2-connect-to-the-sage-300-cre-server)
  * [Why is my Sage 300 CRE data not syncing?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-why-is-my-sage-data-not-syncing)


The _hh_ _2_ _synchronization client_ is a third-party client application developed by hh2. When the ERP Integrations tool is enabled on a company's Procore account and the tool is configured to work with Sage 300 CRE®CRE, your Procore point of contact will work with your Procore Administrator to install and configure the hh2 synchronization client on your Sage 300 CRE® Server. This allows data to sync between your Sage 300 CRE® database server and Procore.
title: Do I need to create a dedicated user account in Sage 300 CRE for the hh2 synchronization client?
description: Although it’s technically not a requirement, it is strongly recommended that you create a new user and do not use an existing user’s credentials.
